metadata.title: The Box Under the Floor
metadata.setting: A small English town where a young man takes a weekend job cleaning an old abandoned house that holds a long-buried secret

characters.name: Tom Reed
characters.description: A slim, dark-haired 19-year-old who is naturally curious and always needs to know the reason for things.
characters.flaw: Too curious — he cannot leave a mystery alone, even when it costs him time or causes trouble
characters.current_state: standing on the path outside the house, unsettled and curious

characters.name: Mrs. Cole
characters.description: A businesslike woman in her sixties with short gray hair who manages the abandoned property on behalf of its new owner.
characters.current_state: calm and businesslike, has locked up the house

locations.name: The abandoned house
locations.description: A two-storey stone house on the edge of a small English town, empty for decades, with dusty rooms, creaking wooden floors, and old furniture left behind.

locations.name: Tom's rented room
locations.description: A small, plain room above a shop in the town centre where Tom lives alone.

episode_history.summary: Tom arrived at the abandoned house to clean the upstairs bedroom and discovered an old video camera hidden in a wooden cupboard. Watching the tape, he saw a frightened family hide a small metal box beneath a loose floorboard in that same room. Before Tom could lift the board, Mrs. Cole arrived early; Tom left the camera on the windowsill, handed back the house key, and was let out, the floorboard still closed and the box still hidden beneath it.

unresolved_threads: Who is the family on the tape, what did they hide in the metal box, and why did they look so afraid?
last_scene_position: Tom is standing on the path outside the abandoned house (loc_001), looking up at the upstairs bedroom window after being let out by Mrs. Cole. Inside the bedroom, the video camera sits on the windowsill where Tom left it, and the loose floorboard in the centre of the room remains undisturbed with the metal box still hidden beneath it. Unresolved: Tom does not know who the frightened family on the tape was or what is inside the box.

series_plan.central_question: What happened to the family in the video, and what secret does the hidden metal box hold?

arc_beats.beat: Tom finds a video camera in a dusty cupboard; the tape shows the same room fifty years ago with a family hiding a metal box under the floorboards; Tom finds the loose board but is forced to leave before he can look beneath it.
Tom returns and lifts the loose floorboard, finding the locked metal box; he notices the name 'Hartley' on a label inside the camera case, giving the family an identity.
An elderly neighbour tells Tom that a young boy named Danny Hartley disappeared from this house in 1974 and was never found; a stranger arrives in town and begins asking questions about the property.
The stranger — an elderly woman — reveals she is Danny's sister and believes the box holds proof of what really happened to her brother; the box is still locked and no key has been found.

Tom watches the tape again and spots a detail he missed before — a small key taped to the inside of the camera case; he opens the box and finds a handwritten letter and a torn document that reveal the truth about Danny.
Tom shares the contents of the box with Danny's sister; the full truth is understood, a long injustice is named, and Tom must decide what to do with the evidence.
